DISCOVERY 610, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL A FURTHER RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 209.2 IS GRANTED IN PART. THE OBJECTIONS, OTHER THAN OVER-BREADTH AS TO SCOPE, ARE OVERRULED AND STRICKEN. AS DRAFTED, THE QUESTION DOES NOT IMPLICATE THE ATTORNEY CLIENT OR ATTORNEY WORK-PRODUCT PRIVILEGE. THE INTERROGATORY IS MODIFIED TO REQUEST THE SPECIFIED INFORMATION FOR THE PAST 10 YEARS IN THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER. ALL REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Nov-09-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sonya Smallets for plaintiff and moving party Katheleen Carroll
Jessica Perry for defendant and opposing party Dorsey & Whitney LLP

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
Plaintiff's motion to quash deposition subpoenas is granted in part. The subpoena to the Department of Fair Employment and Housing are modified to only require production of any records concerning plaintiff that resulted in a court filing, complaint to any federal, state, or local governmental agency, and / or complaint pursuant to the Department's personnel policy relating to disability discrimination, wrongful termination in violation of the public policy against disability discrimination or in violation of the public policy against unethical or illegal billing practices, and / or intentional infliction of emotional distress on the basis of plaintiff's disability. Any names, addresses, or other identifying information of third parties is ordered redacted.

The subpoena to the McGeorge School of Law is similarly modified and shall also permit production of any documents regarding plaintiff's participation in the Career Development Office. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-31-2005 Continued To Jul-22-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion To To Quash The Subpoenas To The Custodians Of Records For California Pacific Medical Center, Dr. Ernest Haeusslein, Dr. James Hershon And Genentech Inc.
Off Calendar May-03-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sonya Smallets for plaintiff and moving party
Jessica Perry for defendant and opposing party
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modifications: Plaintiff's motion to quash subpoenas is denied in part and granted in part. The motion is denied as to the subpoenas for medical records. The subpoena for employment records from Genentech is modified to require production of any records concerning plaintiff that resulted in a court filing, complaint to any federal, state, or local governmental agency, or complaint pursuant to Genentech personnel policy relating to disability discrimination, wrongful termination, or intentional infliction of emotional distress. The names, addresses, or other identifying information of third parties is ordered redacted.

MODIFICATIONS: that only medical records after 1995 need be produced. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
